This example dates to 2000 and is in immaculate condition , with its box and booklets set. #23 - The Rolex GMT-Master II "Pepsi" 16710 w/ Box & Booklets Circa 2000 The History— The Example The Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most iconic watches ever made, originally developed in the 1950s for airline pilots to track multiple time zones during long-haul flights. Its blue and red “Pepsi” bezel quickly became synonymous with international travel, offering a clear day/night distinction at a glance. The reference 16710 GMT-Master II is widely regarded as one of the best executions of the model, perfectly balancing vintage proportions with modern practicality. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3185 , it features an independently adjustable hour hand, allowing the wearer to track a second time zone with ease while maintaining accurate home time – a genuinely useful function for travel. This example dates to 2000 is presented in immaculate condition , with excellent case definition and an immaculate cosmetic appearance. It is accompanied by its box and booklets , completing a strong and highly wearable example. One of the true modern classic wristwatches – functional, iconic and increasingly collectible.
